news-korea-econ.title: South Korea, Ukraine Step Up to Defend Strait of Hormuz as Most of Europe Balks
news-korea-econ.description:
South Korea, France, and Ukraine discussed taking steps to ensure the Strait of Hormuz is safe for commercial ships.

news-global-econ.title: Trump Signs Executive Order Limiting the Years of Eligibility, Number of Transfers
news-global-econ.description:
President Trump has signed an executive order (EO) intended to reform and structure college sports, primarily by limiting the number of years a player is eligible and the number of transfers.

news-global-econ.title: Healthy patients allegedly lured into $50M hospice scam as feds expose cash kickbacks and fake care
news-global-econ.description: Federal authorities in Los Angeles arrest eight in alleged $50 million hospice fraud scheme that prosecutors say turned Medicare into a cash grab.

news-global-econ.title: Nebraska Senate candidate restructures campaign after complaint over payments to family: report
news-global-econ.description: Dan Osborn is reportedly restructuring his Nebraska Senate campaign after a complaint alleged his operation improperly steered money to his wife and other relatives through PACs and consulting firms.

news-global-econ.title: US strikes deep inside Iran, Pentagon shakeup rocks army leadership | Fox News Video
news-global-econ.description: Fox News chief national security correspondent Jennifer Griffin details a Pentagon leadership shakeup, with Gen. Christopher LaNeve named acting Army chief, and the ongoing Iranian threats to civilian vessels on 'The Faulkner Focus.'

news-global-soc.description:

news-global-soc.title: Opportunists: Amazon Tacks on 3.5% Fuel Surcharge for Sellers
news-global-soc.description:
Amazon is implement a new fuel surcharge affecting third-party sellers who rely on its fulfillment network. The online giant will charge sellers an additional 3.5 percent fee to account for what it calls "elevated costs in fuel and logistics."
